title: How do batching and caching affect your latency budget?
prompt:
How do batching and caching affect your latency budget?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Caching is almost universally beneficial: if 30% of queries are semantically similar (common in production), a semantic cache cuts average latency and cost by 30%. Exact-match caching is simple; semantic caching (matching similar queries via embeddings) catches more duplicates but adds complexity. Batching increases throughput but adds latency per request because each waits for the batch to fill — only use for background/async tasks, never for real-time user requests.

**Takeaway:** Cache aggressively for real-time requests. Batch for background tasks only. Design cache keys carefully.
hint_notes: Cache aggressively for real-time requests. Batch for background tasks only. Design cache keys carefully.
